paper_title: A novel method for dendrochronology of large historical wooden objects using line trajectory X-ray tomography

methods.description: This method uses X-ray absorption imaging to visualize tree rings in wooden objects, overcoming the limitation of inaccessible transverse sections. It involves using CT scans to reconstruct 3D volumes from 2D projections, allowing for the visualization of tree rings.

steps.description: Visualize tree rings using X-ray absorption imaging due to density differences within the wood.
steps.searchable_summary: use X-ray absorption imaging to visualize tree rings

steps.description: Perform CT scan by taking radiographs over a 360° rotation circle to reconstruct a 3D volume from 2D projections.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CT scan to reconstruct 3D volume from 2D projections

steps.description: Use iterative tomographic reconstruction methods for non-standard acquisition trajectories.
steps.searchable_summary: apply iterative tomographic reconstruction for non-standard trajectories

methods.description: This method involves scanning wooden objects using a linear movement between the X-ray source and detector to capture tree rings, which are curved surfaces along the longitudinal axis of the tree.

steps.description: Perform line trajectory scanning by moving the object linearly between the source and detector.
steps.searchable_summary: perform line trajectory scanning with linear movement

steps.description: Align the longitudinal axis of the tree with the source-detector axis for sharp imaging of tree rings.
steps.searchable_summary: align longitudinal axis with source-detector axis for sharp imaging

methods.method_name: Scanning Procedure and Analysis of Wooden Test Objects
methods.description: This method describes the scanning procedure and analysis of wooden test objects, including setting up the scan, processing the data, and measuring tree-ring widths.

steps.description: Place wooden test objects on the sample stage, aligning their center with the center of the stage.
steps.searchable_summary: align wooden test objects on sample stage

steps.description: Determine source power, energy, and exposure time for sufficient contrast by visually inspecting projections.
steps.searchable_summary: set source parameters for optimal contrast

steps.description: Calculate the distance the object moves between projections to ensure minimal movement per detector pixel.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate object movement distance between projections

steps.description: Process scan data with flat-and darkfield correction and use Flexbox software with SIRT algorithm for reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: process scan data with corrections and reconstruct using SIRT

steps.description: Prepare transverse surface for dendrochronological research by cleaning and applying chalk powder.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare surface with cleaning and chalk for dendrochronology

steps.description: Photograph the prepared surface with a digital camera in macro mode.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph surface with digital camera in macro mode

steps.description: Measure tree-ring widths on photographs and selected slices using CooRecorder & Cdendro software.
steps.searchable_summary: measure tree-ring widths using CooRecorder & Cdendro

steps.description: Crossdate tree-ring series using PAST4 software, performing statistical tests for overlapping positions.
steps.searchable_summary: crossdate tree-ring series with PAST4 software
